issue_body: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/contrib/go/src/python/pants/contrib/go/tasks/go_thrift_gen.py#L163-L167 will put the workdir at the directory that contains the first source file. If not all of the source files are in the same directory, this will cause the remaining source files to be missed when compiling.

issue_title: Allow PrepareResources task to jar up resources files
issue_body:
To improve perf on junit, jvm run, or similar goals, we want to zip up all the resources files into a jar and then put the jar in the classpath instead of the files and directories directly - classloader is much faster that way.

requirements:
- Make PrepareResources able to jar up resources files from Resources target.
- By default off in OSS since no one else raise the need yet.
- Be able to shorten the resulting jar file path to avoid hitting OS arg length limit.
- Be able to allow individual Resources target to opt out.

issue_title: Starter project - version of 'globs/rglobs' that uses regular python regular expression syntax
issue_body:
'rglobs()' just barely works, you can't specify a complex expression (like a prefix before the '*') and expect it to work, see https://github.com/twitter/commons/issues/380 

Ideally we'd fix the broken underlying function but I've tried it and its not simple!

But I was thinking that it should be pretty easy to make a new Fileset type that respected python regular expression syntax.  Then you could be reasonably certain that any type of expression would work.  You could make a version of globs:

```
a_target(name='foo',
  sources=search(r'<regexp>'),
...
)
```

and a version of rglobs:

```
a_target(name='foo',
  sources=rsearch(r'<regexp>'), 
  ...
)
```

issue_title: let a python_dist() depend on a python_library()
issue_body:
`python_library()` targets can depend on `python_dist()` targets, but not the other way around. After a short discussion in `#pex` today @jsirois provided a link to [`ExportedTargetDependencyCalculator` in `setup_py.py`](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/78b039e2c08818d57851a7e78596f686fb7fc61d/src/python/pants/backend/python/tasks/setup_py.py#L122), and said:

> Yeah, see the model in SetupPy. The graph is walked and and dependencies that do not provides are sucked in, if they do provides, they're added as `install_requires`.

So the logic to do everything we need (add an `install_requires` for a target with a `provides`, or copy over sources from `python_library()` targets without a `provides`) is already in `setup_py.py`, and could probably be adapted pretty easily.

I would want a `python_dist()` to be able to depend on python requirements as well to resolve this ticket.

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: 2010-09-09

issue_title: content descriptions are unsafely rendered as raw HTML
issue_body:
### Observed behavior

The [description](https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri/blob/develop/kolibri/plugins/learn/assets/src/views/content-page/index.vue#L46) is rendered as [raw HTML](https://vuejs.org/v2/guide/syntax.html#Raw-HTML).

### Expected behavior

All user-generated content should be sanitized.

### User-facing consequences

Our users are susceptible to XSS

### Open question

Are any users relying on this bug/feature? Would removing it break existing content?

issue_title: add documentation in code and developer docs indicating that APIs are internal
issue_body:
### Observed behavior

We're starting to see people in the community build external apps for integrating with Kolibri, either by asking us for documentation or by inspecting the source directly.

### Expected behavior

We should dissuade people from building third party apps on the current internal API, and make clear that a stable, public API is on our roadmap.

### User-facing consequences

If third-party devs create apps under the impression that our internal APIs are stable, they will be frustrated when those apps break because we updated the APIs.

issue_title: Lookup for some language codes returned by YouTube api fail
issue_body:
When using [YouTubeSubtitleFile](https://github.com/learningequality/ricecooker/blob/master/ricecooker/classes/files.py#L423), some language codes still fail or are resolved to inaccurate.

  - Failed: `iw` (old code for Hebrew), should be `he`
  - Possibly inaccurate: `zh-Hans` (simpflified Chinese) should be `zh-CN` 
  - Possibly inaccurate: `zh-Hant` (traditional Chinese) should be `zh-TW`


### Possible workaround

Add special handlers in `getlang_by_alpha2` to handle these edge cases.

We can't know know which other non-standard language codes are used by YouTube API so we'll have to implement such fixes on a case by case basis...

issue_title: TypeError: __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'encoding'
issue_body:
`html5lib.version is 1.0.1` produces the error

```
read_html('http://foo.com')

TypeError: __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'encoding'

```
Solution is to downgrade:`pip3 install -U html5lib=="0.9999999"`

It's a problem on SO [here](https://stackoverflow.com/q/46499808/3281978): and [here](https://stackoverflow.com/q/46499808/3281978)

It looks like it was referenced [as another issue but was closed](https://github.com/html5lib/html5lib-python/issues/281
)

Is it possible to fix this in version 1.0.1?

issue_title: alphabeticalattributes filter TypeError when comparing namespaced attributes with non-namespaced attributes
issue_body:
In SVG 1.1, you could have something like this:

```
<svg><pattern id="patt1" xlink:href="http://example.com/#whatever" /></svg>
```

In Python 3, that creates the following attributes:

```
dict_items([((None, 'id'), 'patt1'), (('http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink', 'href'), '#patt2')])
```

The problem is that this means we're comparing `None` with a str here:

https://github.com/html5lib/html5lib-python/blob/17499b9763a090f7715af49555d21fe4b558958b/html5lib/filters/alphabeticalattributes.py#L17

That kicks up a TypeError.

Script:

```
import html5lib
from html5lib.serializer import HTMLSerializer


text = '<svg><pattern id="patt1" xlink:href="http://example.com/#foo" /></svg>'

parser = html5lib.HTMLParser()
dom = parser.parseFragment(text)
walker = html5lib.getTreeWalker('etree')
ser = HTMLSerializer(alphabetical_attributes=True)

ser.render(walker(dom))
```

Traceback:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "foo.py", line 12, in <module>
    ser.render(walker(dom))
  File "/home/willkg/mozilla/bleach/.tox/py34-html5lib99999999/lib/python3.4/site-packages/html5lib/serializer.py", line 323, in render
    return "".join(list(self.serialize(treewalker)))
  File "/home/willkg/mozilla/bleach/.tox/py34-html5lib99999999/lib/python3.4/site-packages/html5lib/serializer.py", line 209, in serialize
    for token in treewalker:
  File "/home/willkg/mozilla/bleach/.tox/py34-html5lib99999999/lib/python3.4/site-packages/html5lib/filters/optionaltags.py", line 18, in __iter__
    for previous, token, next in self.slider():
  File "/home/willkg/mozilla/bleach/.tox/py34-html5lib99999999/lib/python3.4/site-packages/html5lib/filters/optionaltags.py", line 9, in slider
    for token in self.source:
  File "/home/willkg/mozilla/bleach/.tox/py34-html5lib99999999/lib/python3.4/site-packages/html5lib/filters/alphabeticalattributes.py", line 17, in __iter__
    key=lambda x: x[0]):
TypeError: unorderable types: str() < NoneType()
```

This is true of html5lib 0.99999999 (8 9s) and 0.999999999 (9 9s).

issue_title: In lieu of using settings for options, we should consider app-specific settings...
issue_body:
...that are managed on the front end, via toolbar menus.

`ALDRYN_NEWSBLOG_CREATE_AUTHOR` and `ALDRYN_NEWSBLOG_PAGINATE_BY` should both be per-app-config and *not* specified in settings.

issue_title: charm-tools as of 2.0 pulls in x11
issue_body:
This appears to be due to mercurial pulling in tcl, which pulls in x11. 

These recommends directives are bloating our tooling and imagery with x11-common, tcl, and other depends we don't need. Can we change these to --no-recommends, or perhaps consider another method to reduce the overall incoming packages?

issue_title: Make it easier to extract a random FASTA subset, just by giving a target fraction
issue_body: The `randomSubset` filtering is fine, but we don't need that precision in some cases and it's a hassle to have to work out the number of reads. We could use reservoir sampling (which needs the sample size to be not too large, as the sample has to be held in memory). But simpler, it would be good to just say something like `sampleFraction=0.1` to filter approximately 10% of the reads. Then you can take similar (proportionally) samples without needing to care about the number of reads present.

issue_title: Non-deterministic results because of aggregation steps
issue_body:
Because the aggregation step is not deterministic, the data may be presented in different orders between runs of the same query. A number of algorithms (DBSCAN) will then give different results because.

The recommended solution is to sort all the data point by lexicographic order before fitting them:
```
sorted_data = data[numpy.lexsort(data.T)]
```

issue_title: Connecting from behind a proxy
issue_body:
in your OTXv2.py module, within the get function, you have the ProxyHandler set to http:

```
if self.proxy:
            proxy = ProxyHandler({'http': self.proxy})
```

If the user sets the proxy value, this will cause an error because the url being called out to is https:

`server="https://otx.alienvault.com"`

Updating the OTXv2.py module to 'https' rather than 'http' fixes the issue:
```

if self.proxy:
            proxy = ProxyHandler({'https': self.proxy})
```

issue_title: Support globstar (**) patterns in .dockerignore
issue_body:
This is a disparity between docker-py and the Engine CLI. Patterns in `.dockerignore` containing `**` don't match correctly - they should recursively match all files and subdirectories.

Reported in https://github.com/docker/compose/issues/1607#issuecomment-206515434 and https://github.com/docker/compose/issues/1607#issuecomment-229829359.

issue_title: BayesianModel.fit() method gives conditional prob. = 0.5 if conditioned on single variable
issue_body:
### Subject of the issue
After using .fit() method, **conditional probability conditioned on single variable, such as P(Z|X) is always 0.5**
**Conditioned on two variables, such as P(Z|X,Y) gives correct result.**

### Your environment
* pgmpy version 0.1.6
* Python version 3.6.4
* Operating System Windows

### Steps to reproduce
[example-1]
print ('\n Model-1: P(Z|X)')
from pgmpy.models import BayesianModel
from pgmpy.estimators import MaximumLikelihoodEstimator

data = {'X': [0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 1], 'Z': [0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1, 1]}
data_xz = pd.DataFrame(data)
model_xz = BayesianModel([('X','Z')])
model_xz.fit(data_xz, estimator=MaximumLikelihoodEstimator)
print (model_xz.get_cpds('Z'))

[example-2]
print ('\n Model-2: P(Z|X,Y)')
from pgmpy.models import BayesianModel
from pgmpy.estimators import MaximumLikelihoodEstimator

data = {'X': [0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 1], 'Y': [0, 0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 1], 'Z': [0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1, 1]}
data_xyz1 = pd.DataFrame(data)
model_xyz1 = BayesianModel([('X','Z'), ('Y','Z')])
model_xyz1.fit(data_xyz1, estimator=MaximumLikelihoodEstimator)
print (model_xyz1.get_cpds('Z'))

### Expected behaviour
[example-1] P(Z|X=0) = 0.25, P(Z|X=1) = 0.75
[example-2] P(Z|X=0, Y=0) = 0, P(Z|X=0, Y=1) = 0.5, P(Z|X=1, Y=0) = 0.5, P(Z|X=1, Y=0) = 1

### Actual behaviour
Conditional probability, conditioned on single variable, is wrong.
[example-1] P(Z|X=0) = 0.5, P(Z|X=1) = 0.5 --> wrong
[example-2] P(Z|X=0, Y=0) = 0, P(Z|X=0, Y=1) = 0.5, P(Z|X=1, Y=0) = 0.5, P(Z|X=1, Y=0) = 1 --> correct !!

issue_title: Support methods in Channels Context Manager
issue_body:
Builds on https://github.com/ni/nimi-python/issues/185

Under this proposal, the context manager would have a subset of the methods Session has, specifically those that have a channel input. But the channel parameter would be removed.

So this:

    my_session.set_level("0,1", 3.3)
    voltage = my_session.read("0,1")

becomes

    with my_session.channel("0,1") as channel_context:
        channel_context.set_level(3.3)
        voltage = channel_context.read()

issue_title: Remove PYTHONHASHSEED checks
issue_body: The original point of the `PYTHONHASHSEED` checks was to ensure repeatability of results.  Pytorch is inherently non-deterministic, though, because of CUDNN (see #387), so it doesn't seem like requiring `PYTHONHASHSEED` to have a fixed value does us any good.  It's just another hoop to jump through before using the library, and it should be removed.

issue_title: Remove preprended underscores from files in the serialization directory.
issue_body: The underscores were from a time when the `serialization_prefix` wasn't necessarily a directory.  They should be removed.  This is for these files: `_model_params.json`, `_stdout.log`, `_stderr.log`, and `_python_logging.log`.

issue_title: The demo database is not aware of new model versions
issue_body: If you have a query which is in present in the database, it's going to return old model results rather than run the new model. We should add a column to the database which records the model version it used and bust the cache if it doesn't match the current model version.

issue_title: Make some of the dependencies optional for prediction
issue_body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I am building docker images which do online predictions using pretrained allennlp models. However there are numerous dependencies of allennlp which significantly slow down the building of the images and also make the size of image unnecessarily big.  Those dependencies include spacy, nltk, scipy, scikit-learn, aws-cli, etc. Although these are great and useful tools but in our application we either use some alternative tools or do them in upstream components. For example we found spacy 2.0 significantly slower than 1.x so we still stick to 1.x in our components.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Can we make some of the requirements optional so that pip doesn't install them all. And then users can choose to install if necessary. This may involve changing those import to inline so that they are imported only when the components are called.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I was thinking to use pip uninstall to uninstall some of them but it's a little hacky and I am not sure if it will break those imports.

issue_title: FYI: OVITO Trajectories
issue_body:
http://ovito.org/manual/howto.visualize_particle_trajectories.html

FYI:
OVITO can visualize trajectory lines of individual particles as shown in the example movie on the right. A trajectory object is created using the Create trajectory lines utility as described in the following.

Procedure

Figure 10. The Create trajectory lines utility.

The Create trajectory lines utility.

The user interface of the utility (Figure 10) lets you select the set of particles for which to create trajectory lines (all or selected ones) and the time span over which the trajectory lines should be generated. The Unwrap trajectories option is useful when particles cross the boundaries of a periodic simulation cell.

Once you have made all settings, press the Create trajectory lines button. OVITO will now load each simulation frame one after another, sample the particle positions, and generate the trajectory lines. They are stored in a Particle trajectories object by OVITO, which is a new object added to the scene. The new trajectories object is automatically selected in the modification panel.

Figure 11. The trajectory display settings.

The trajectory display settings.

You can switch back and forth between the Trajectories and the Particles object using the scene object selector box (marked by a red circle in Figure 11). Alternatively, you can simpy click on a particle or on a trajectory line in the viewports to select the corresponding object.

The trajectory object is linked to the particles object it was created from. You may use the Regenerate trajectory lines button to update the trajectories if necessary, e.g., if the input simulation data has changed.

Procedure summary: You first use the Create trajectory lines utility to create a new trajectory object from the currently loaded simulation time series. You may then adjust the display settings for the newly created trajectory object as shown in Figure 11. Finally, you can switch back to the Particles object to continue working with the particles data, apply modifiers etc. The trajectory object is not affected by any changes made to the particles data unless you press the Regenerate trajectory lines button of the trajectory object.

Notes

issue_title: Should add support for metrics collection from all processors and pipelines
issue_body:
## Scenario 1 - long running processor
* Run a long-running processor
* Want to see the progress of the processor as it runs

#### expected
* should have a way to quickly, reliably and consistently check the progress
* Some example questions:
  * how many rows were processed so far?
  * which processor is currently running? for how long?

### actual
* try to track the output data - but it might be committed in batches, also it's not a reliable or consistent.
* check the processor log (add logging.info) - also, not reliable or consistent


## Scenario 2 - BI / reporting
* Have a complex environment with many pipelines
* Want to see reports showing which pipelines / processors ran and when / show statistics

#### expected
* should have a way to generate reports over time
* example questsions:
  * what's the average processing time per pipeline / per processor / per row
  * when a pipeline last ran in the past? for how long it ran? how many rows were yielded?

#### actual
* only possible by looking at the output data

## Scenario 3 - alerting
* Have a complex environment with many pipelines
* it's hard to track pipelines manually - should have automatic alerts based on real time data

#### expected
* should have a way to alert in real-time based on pipeline processing
* example alerts:
  * a pipeline takes too long to run (compared to previous average running time)
  * a pipeline is "stuck" and stopped yielding new rows in a timely manner

#### actual
* no way to have real-time alerts

## Suggested solution

The [datapackage-pipelines-metrics](https://github.com/orihoch/datapackage-pipelines-metrics) provides most of the required features, but it has 2 major problems:

* it's not pretty to integrate - you have to rename all pipeline specs to the plugin source spec filename
  * or - manually add it to your own source spec - or to each pipeline
* it aggregates metrics based on datapackage or resource - there is no way to analyze a specific step or a specific processor

AFAIK the current plugin framework doesn't support this use-case - which might be a good thing (I wouldn't like to see general hooks / events which allow to modify how the system works)

but - I think that for metrics, it is needed

perhaps a generic pluggable metrics architecture is in order?

issue_body:
The IESO (Ontario) has archived very old content in CSV reports. 

http://www.ieso.ca/en/power-data/data-directory

They don't get updated as regularly as the public market operations reports (i.e. the XML reports currently being parsed by `IESOClient`) but we could extend the historical range for `get_trade(...)` and `get_load(...)` by stitching the CSV content together with the existing content parsed from the existing XML reports. Notably, we could parse:

- `get_trade(...)` from start of year to the lower bound of XML report content: http://www.ieso.ca/-/media/files/ieso/uploaded/market-reports/hourlyimportexportschedules.csv?la=en
- `get_trade(...)` for 2002 to the end of "last year": http://www.ieso.ca/-/media/files/ieso/power-data/data-directory/hourlyimportexportschedules_2002-2016.csv?la=en
- `get_load(...)` from start of year to the lower bound of XML report content: http://www.ieso.ca/-/media/files/ieso/uploaded/market-reports/hourlydemands.csv?la=en
- `get_load(...)` from 2002 to the end of "last year": http://www.ieso.ca/-/media/files/ieso/power-data/data-directory/hourlydemands_2002-2016.csv?la=en
- `get_load(...)` from 1994 through 2001: http://www.ieso.ca/-/media/files/ieso/power-data/data-directory/hourlydemands_1994-2002.csv?la=en

issue_title: Clarification: how scalable is deis-router?
issue_body:
`deisctl` permits the `deis-router` component to be "scaled," and comments in the `deisctl` code specifically call out `deis-registry` as being one of the few components that can currently scale.

But when I look at unit files for deployed applications, they all get the registry address from the etcd keey `/deis/registry/host`.

So, I'm looking for some small bit of clarification here.  Am I missing something?  Or does "scalable" here actually have more to do with creating warm standbys than it does with scaling capacity?  If additional registries are nothing but warm standbys, what advantage (if any) does creating those offer over, say, simply preseeding all nodes with the Deis component images?

If nothing else, there's an opportunity to address this in the documentation, which I am happy to work on once I understand this better.

issue_title: clarify usage of ps:restart
issue_body:
`deis help ps` reads as "restart process types (e.g. web, worker)" which implies that you can only restart a specific process type. The documentation at `deis help ps:restart` also shows "Restarts an application's processes by type" which is untrue. It allows you to restart all containers, a specific process type, or an individual container:

```
deis ps:restart # restarts all processes
deis ps:restart web # restarts all web processes
deis ps:restart web.1 # restarts web.1
```

We should clarify this in the client help.

issue_title: bug(router): domains with sub-subdomains isn't matched anymore
New Dockerfile+Procfile behavior broken
issue_body:
We had deployed a cluster with Deis v1.0.1 following the guidance in [gh#2545](https://github.com/deis/deis/issues/2545) for the Dockerfile + Procfile behavior at the time.

For that, we had the Dockerfile CMD start a script that prepares application environment variables. If it detects $1 as the proctype ("start web" would pass "web", for example) and would run foreman start on that proctype, otherwise it would execute $@ outright.

This was logical. The Dockerfile had a CMD, and Deis honored it. It wasn't pretty, but it wasn't horrible either.

We just deployed a cluster with Deis v1.3.1, which no longer honors the CMD script defined in the Dockerfile.

Instead, it seems that [pr2605](https://github.com/deis/deis/pull/2605) has the scheduler scanning the procfile to find the command to run.

This is conceptually the correct behavior. However, the generated unit isn't being executed in a pure shell context.

Case in point, here's our Procfile:

    web: bundle exec puma -t ${PUMA_MIN_THREADS:-16}:${PUMA_MAX_THREADS:-16} -w ${PUMA_WORKERS:-8} -p ${PORT:-3000} -e ${RAILS_ENV:-development} --preload

And here is the unit that Deis v1.3.1's fleet scheduler now generates:

    [Unit]
    Description=myapp_v5.web.1
    
    [Service]
    ExecStartPre=/bin/sh -c "IMAGE=$(etcdctl get /deis/registry/host 2>&1):$(etcdctl get /deis/registry/port 2>&1)/,myapp:v5; docker pull $IMAGE"
    ExecStartPre=/bin/sh -c "docker inspect myapp_v5.web.1 >/dev/null 2>&1 && docker rm -f myapp_v5.web.1 || true"
    ExecStart=/bin/sh -c "IMAGE=$(etcdctl get /deis/registry/host 2>&1):$(etcdctl get /deis/registry/port 2>&1)/myapp:v5; port=$(docker inspect -f '{{range $k, $v := .ContainerConfig.ExposedPorts }}{{$k}}{{end}}' $IMAGE | cut -d/ -f1) ; docker run --name myapp.web.1 -m 3g   -P -e PORT=$port $IMAGE bash -c 'bundle exec puma -t ${PUMA_MIN_THREADS:-16}:${PUMA_MAX_THREADS:-16} -w ${PUMA_WORKERS:-8} -p ${PORT:-3000} -e ${RAILS_ENV:-development} --preload'"
    ExecStop=/usr/bin/docker rm -f myapp_v5.web.1
    TimeoutStartSec=20m
    RestartSec=5
    Restart=on-failure

The shell script quoting looks right. But this is a systemd service. As we all know from painful first-hand experience, those variable expansions happen differently there.

When I look at the running process that is failing to start using ps, I see this:

    docker run --name myapp.web.1 -m 3g -P -e PORT=3000 10.113.1.220:5000/cosmos-mother:v5 bash -c bundle exec puma -t : -w  -p  -e  --preload

That's right. All of those ${} expansions are being eaten by systemd.

My problem here is that now I can't simply backslash the $'s in the Procfile and not break my other environments (nor should I have to).

I still have this stuff running on developer workstations using docker-expose/fig, which correctly runs the CMD entrypoint script of the Dockerfile to start the container there.

I also have this deployed to that old deis v1.0.1 cluster, which likewise would break if I do the same.

Instead, I now have to change my Procfile to look like this:

    web: ./bin/web.sh

And in bin/web.sh in the project I now have this:

    #$!/bin/bash
    set -eo pipefail
    
    # re-export the environment variables for the next process
    export $(env | cut -d= -f1)
    
    proc="exec bundle exec puma -t ${PUMA_MIN_THREADS:-16}:${PUMA_MAX_THREADS:-16} -w ${PUMA_WORKERS:-8} -p ${PORT:-3000} -e ${RAILS_ENV:-development} --preload"
    
    [[ -x /run.sh ]] &&
      /run.sh "$proc"
    else
      $proc
    fi

This "hides" those variables from systemd and lets them evaluate in a shell context as they should be.

It would be great if we could default to running the Dockerfile CMD by default as it has been in the past, but allow that to be overridden by a later deis client command for a given app and proctype if the devops so desires.

As mentioned above, I've worked around this. No doubt others WILL encounter this problem, and this WILL be a support issue for someone other than myself, it's just a matter of time.

You've already partially reverted this behavior at least for heroku buildpacks in [gh#2673](https://github.com/deis/deis/pull/2673).

What does OpDemand think?

issue_title: deis blocks domain creation under improper circumstances
issue_body:
`$ deis domains:add django.paas-sandbox`
`Adding django.paas-sandbox to rubber-undertow... 400 BAD REQUEST`
`{u'domain': [u'Hostname does not look like a valid hostname. Only lowercase characters are allowed.']}`

deis will not let me create a domain where the top of the domain is "paas-sandbox", as displayed above, however this is not a top level domain nor is it intended as one. Our corporation uses an internal domain that is set as the dns search on all corporate computers. For example, if someone types `http://www/` in their browser, it will come up because their dns searches `corp.example.com` for non qualifying domains. They are really going to the site `www.corp.example.com` but all they type and all the host header on the server sees is "www". deis requires the domain to exist how the user will display it in the browser in order to render the page. Our DNS system is almost identical to Active Directory so anyone using Active Directory can relate but this is not a MS / Active Directory specific scenario. This can be done using only dhcp and most ISP's will do this from their dhcp as well.

Anyways, please fix. Thank you.

Note I am on a stateless V1.13.2 cluster with CoreOS 1068.8.0 and applied fix #5074 (although I do not think that matters)

So I see 2 probably distinct bugs

A) It crashes on a container restart (this seems to be consistent and we get a stacktrace so probably quite easy to debug and solve). At minimum this will give us some missing logs during container restarts (probably the boot logs of a container which is very frustrating if that container is crashing on boot and there is no easy way to see the logs)

B) It does not seem to attach to containers on startup (this is without logs and does not happen every time, I suspect some sort of race condition in the boot sequence)

If there is anything I can do to help debug this issue (eg try out a custom logspout image) I am obviously willing to do so

issue_title: Parallelization performance v0.7.1 and commit that ends with 1c99e8
issue_body:
Hi,

Two topics that I wanted to discuss:

##  Runtime increase in latest commit
We compared the extraction time of [version 0.7.1](https://github.com/blue-yonder/tsfresh/releases/tag/v0.7.1) to code version [1c99e8](https://github.com/blue-yonder/tsfresh/commit/76b27dd95b7702c1c0222d55aca755ab5b1c99e8). We varied the number of ids ,where every chunk/time-series had length = 91 and the dataframe had 4 kinds of ts columns. As you may see in the figure and table below, we didn't see any significant improvement when using 4 processes in parallel and `CHUNCKSIZE = None` (fastest settings). How does this fit your [benchmark results](https://github.com/blue-yonder/tsfresh/pull/217) ?

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13464827/28775204-6f5ab9c4-75f9-11e7-8e6f-7010994b596e.png)

Results table:

#ids | 0.7.1 w parallel | 1c99e8 w parallel
-- | -- | --
940 | 8.95 ±1.2 | 14.6 ±2.47
4500 | 46.98 ±14.21 | 61.96 ±1.66
7612 | 74.37 ±9.18 | 69.07 ±6.3
30523 | 295.98 ±49.15 | 317.16 ±16.52

BTW, I actually saw a nice improvement with no-parallelization (`N_PROCESSES = 0`). See table below:

#ids | 0.7.1 w/o parallel | 1c99e8 w/o parallel
-- | -- | --
940 | 12.42 ±0.51 | 10.34±0.69
4500 | 58.04 ±1.04 | 45.79±0.09

*mean and std taken from 5 trials*

##  50% CPU usage with no-parallelization
Recently I've noticed that while using the package for feature extraction, the CPU usage with no-parallelization settings stays mostly near 50±2%. This means that functions that are located deeper in `_do_extraction_on_chunk` are opening many processes/threads that are not configurable by the tsfresh API. Accordingly, what is the meaning of working with more than 2-3 processes, which use 100% CPU?
I've also noticed, that when working with  `N_PROCESSES > 4` run time tends to raise drastically


![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13464827/28774743-8a0338ca-75f7-11e7-92f1-4ff609732b43.png)


### Disclaimer: 
I've modified the parallelization backend in `feature_extraction/extraction.py` from `multiprocess` to` concurrent.futures (ProcessPoolExecuter)` in order to allow an hierarchical parallelization scheme. This scheme allows to run in parallel 2 modules - one module for DB extraction, preprocessing and feature extraction (tsfresh) and second child module for the internal tsfresh parallelization . The change can be seen in the image bellow

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13464827/28774962-626a7228-75f8-11e7-8162-63a680852c51.png)


**Benchmark settings**
OS: win7, 
Resources: Xeon 48-cores, 32 RAM GB
Python interpreter: python 3.6
tsfresh package version: [0.7.1](https://github.com/blue-yonder/tsfresh/releases/tag/v0.7.1) and [1c99e8](https://github.com/blue-yonder/tsfresh/commit/76b27dd95b7702c1c0222d55aca755ab5b1c99e8)
feature extraction settings: mean,std,median,max,min,augmented_dickey_fuller, ar_5_params

Thanks

issue_title: Discussion: How to improve filter api
issue_body:
### What was wrong?

See #836.  Current web3 filter api's are not intuitive and are just unable to do certain things.  An example is creating a filter for array event arguments.  Current argument encoding can not differentiate between argument options and arguments that _are_ arrays:

Current api:

```python
some_contract.events.eventWithArrayArg.createFilter(fromBlock='latest', filter={'array_arg': [b'00', b'11'])
```

The following lines in web3/utils/events.py parse the above array incorrectly into argument options:

```
 67  	    encoded_args = [
 68  	        [
 69  	            None if option is None else encode_hex(encode_single(arg['type'], option))
 70  	            for option in arg_options]
 71  ->	        for arg, arg_options in zipped_abi_and_args
 72  	    ]
```

### How can it be fixed?

Argument values to filter on should have abi type aware handling, so that the filter parameters can be validated, and array type arguments can be distinguished from lists of multiple match targets.  A few ideas for how this could look:

**Option 1**. A filter argument type, that evaluates to the encoded topic or topics.  Can 
be supplied to the topic parameter list when creating an event filter.

```python
my_array_arg_single_value = contract.events.SomeEvent.args['my_array_argument']([b'11', b'22'])
my_array_arg_single_value
b'\xac{\x94\xf1X\x12S\xf5\x02\xa5\xfa \xba\xeb.\xcf<\x8b\xfa\xd2G6\xcbw\xe4,\x84\x99\x0eMew'
    
my_array_arg_multiple_values = contract.events.SomeEvent.args['my_array_argument']([b'00'], [b'11', b'22'])
[b'\xd3R@\xdf\xb7\xd5\xf3\xe7\xa3?i\x87+\x84Y\x04g\xe5\xd4\xce.z3\x12\xff\xa8\xe5\xc5\xab6{\xbe', b'\xac{\x94\xf1X\x12S\xf5\x02\xa5\xfa \xba\xeb.\xcf<\x8b\xfa\xd2G6\xcbw\xe4,\x84\x99\x0eMew']
        
contract.events.SomeEvent.createFilter(fromBlock='latest', topics=[my_array_arg_multiple_values])
```

Although this is a bit awkward, it is nice that the argument object could be immutable.


**Option 2.** The createFilter method to accept kwargs for event arguements

Single value:
```python
contract.events.SomeEvent.createFilter(fromBlock='latest', my_array_argument=[b'11', b'22'])
```    
Mutiple argument values:
```python
contract.events.SomeEvent.createFilter(fromBlock='latest', my_array_argument=[[b'00'], [b'11', b'22']])
```

I like this for its simplicity, although setting multiple values as lists still feels a bit unclear.

**Option 3.** A FilterParameters object that manages parameter checking, that gets passed into createFilter.

```python
params = contract.events.SomeEvent.new_filter_parameters()
params.args['my_array_argument'].match_values([b'00'], [b'11, b'22'])
params.fromBlock = 'latest'
new_filter = contract.events.SomeEvent.createFilter(params)
```

Accepting multiple values as method arguments makes the intention more readable, but having a mutable object manage the parameters is not ideal.

Immediately reject non-`str` values to `decode_hex`

Problem is here:
https://github.com/ethereum/eth-utils/blob/master/eth_utils/currency.py#L11

My application uses other precision `prec=28` but package changes global variable after import. It's not good behavior for libraries.

issue_title: What's package ending with '@' ?
issue_body:
I see several places where packages ending with '@' are being referred to, in conda code:

conda/models/dist.py
```python
 72     def is_feature_package(self):
 73         return self.dist_name.endswith('@')
```

conda/cli/main_search.py
```python
185         if '@' in name:
186             continue
```

tests/test_resolve.py
```python
221     def test_anaconda_mkl_2(self):
222         # to test "with_features_depends"
223         dists = r.install(['anaconda 1.5.0', 'python 2.7*', 'numpy 1.7*', 'mkl@'])
```

I don't see any of '@' or 'with_feature_depends' anywhere in conda docs. Am I looking at the wrong place?

* http://conda.pydata.org/docs/spec.html#package-match-specifications (for '@')
* http://conda.pydata.org/docs/search.html?q=with_features_depends&check_keywords=yes&area=default (for 'with_features_depends')

Can we please have this documented somewhere? Even after going through http://conda.pydata.org/docs/building/bpp.html I couldn't figure out a scenario using conda-build to build a package having 'with_features_depends'

**I'm submitting a...**

```

See current behavior

It should succeed and not fail as it did before https://github.com/conda/conda/commit/7a8455c17fcf922c2d0d9512bea7412b4cf4f2bf introduced https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/master/conda_env/cli/main_remove.py#L54-L56

issue_title: Way to have conda ignore package not found errors on install.
issue_body:
From time to time I find myself needing to do something like: 

    conda install [...long...list...of...packages...]

The first time conda can't find one of the packages the entire process stops. I'd like to be able to tell it to just ignore that one and keep going.

What I end up doing is:

    for pkg in [list of packages]; do conda install -y $pkg; done

which bypasses the problem. This bypasses the problem, but is very slow as many packages end up getting installed as dependencies of previous ones.

issue_title: environment dependent PYTHONSTARTUP file
issue_body:
I currently have a minimal set of imports that I use for most interactive sessions in a `.pythonrc` file at $PYTHONSTARTUP, i.e.:

```python
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
...
```

Which I would like to be environment dependent (as not all envs have numpy etc.).  I have been able to do this by putting this `.pythonrc` in the root of the environment, and setting PYTHONSTARTUP to it in `{ENVROOT}/etc/activate.d/env_vars.sh` (and then unsetting it in `deactivate.d`), but in my opinion, this is unnecessarily clunky and won't port between systems, so could do with specific conda support.   A simple way to set this up would be to have something like a `{ENVROOT}/startup.py` that is run after the script at $PYTHONSTARTUP, allowing the convenience code that typically goes in these files (e.g. https://github.com/whiteinge/dotfiles/blob/master/.pythonrc.py) to be environment independent.  Not sure how the best way to do it would be - thoughts?

I'm afraid I'm currently writing up my PhD so don't have time to dive into the conda code and offer a PR.

issue_title: conda and csh interaction problems
issue_body:
Reported today on the Conda ML, possibly by @mhagerty.  Not my issue, but seems like something to look into:

---

Something weird is indeed going on here.  I am not a bash user, I am a csh user.  So I "activate" with `source ~/anaconda/bin/activate.csh myenv`.  This changes my prompt to reflect the environment change but as we'll see below, it apparently doesn't properly set things up for conda to work.   As you noted, it seems that conda isn't recognizing the activated environment even after it changes my prompt!

Furthermore, if I switch to bash, and do `source activate myenv` and then run `conda install 'pyqt<5'`: rather than saying it's already installed, it installs pyqt4 again!

```
(myenv) bash-3.2$ conda install 'pyqt<5'
Using Anaconda Cloud api site https://api.anaconda.org
Fetching package metadata .........
Solving package specifications: ..........

Package plan for installation in environment /Users/myusername/anaconda/envs/myenv:

The following packages will be downloaded:

    package                    |            build
    ---------------------------|-----------------
    freetype-2.7               |                0         791 KB  conda-forge
    qt-4.8.7                   |                4        37.1 MB
    ------------------------------------------------------------
                                           Total:        37.9 MB

The following NEW packages will be INSTALLED:

    freetype: 2.7-0         conda-forge
    libpng:   1.6.24-0      conda-forge
    pyqt:     4.11.4-py27_0 conda-forge
    qt:       4.8.7-4                 
    sip:      4.18-py27_0   conda-forge

Proceed ([y]/n)?

Fetching packages ...
freetype-2.7-0 100% |###################################################################| Time: 0:00:00   2.32 MB/s
qt-4.8.7-4.tar 100% |###################################################################| Time: 0:00:13   2.86 MB/s
Extracting packages ...
[      COMPLETE      ]|######################################################################################| 100%
Linking packages ...
[      COMPLETE      ]|######################################################################################| 100%
```

...and NOW when I try to import PyQt4, it simply works:

```
>>> import PyQt4
>>>
```

*furthermore*, if I leave bash and go into csh, and activate my anaconda environment again, THEN it also works:

```
>>> import PyQt4
>>>
```

So the lesson here is that conda is only partially compatible with csh, despite there being a script for it.  I will remember: *"always run bash when using conda"*.


Thank you, you have gotten me through this and I can now get back to what I was originally trying to do a few hours ago when PyQt4 turned up missing!

Someone should have a look at the interaction of the `activate.csh` script and/or conda...  I never have trouble when I use `pip`, but apparently there's an issue with "conda".

issue_title: epoch specification (bang !) in versions confuse conda
issue_body:
PEP440 allows to use `!` to specify epochs. This confuses some of the conda commands, because `!` can also be the first char of `!=`.

Some examples follow:

### Specific version installation fails:

    $ conda install -c msys2 m2-mintty=1!12.2.3
    Error: invalid package specification: m2-mintty=1!12.2.3

### `env export` fails if any package has a `!` in its version

    $ conda env export -n m2tty
     Error: invalid package specification: msys2::m2-mintty=1!2.2.3=1

### The regexp of `conda.cli.common.spec_from_line`  is the reason for this:

    >>> from conda.cli import common
    >>> print(common.spec_from_line('msys2::m2-mintty-1!12.2.3'))
    msys2::m2-mintty-1 !12.2.3
    >>> print(common.spec_from_line('msys2::m2-mintty-112.2.3'))
    msys2::m2-mintty-112.2.3

issue_title: conda install --yes flag ignored when --use-local set with conda-build 2.1.6
issue_body:
@Lnaden commented on [Fri Mar 10 2017](https://github.com/conda/conda-build/issues/1825)

Trying to execute `conda install --yes --use-local {package}` from a package built with `conda-build` ignores the `--yes` flag and waits for user input. This [has](https://travis-ci.org/choderalab/openmmtools/jobs/209789665#L951) put a [halt](https://travis-ci.org/choderalab/pymbar/jobs/209453492#L646) to trying to do automatic builds as they time out, I am only using the 2 links as examples.

I can replicate the problem on my local machine and on downgrading to `conda-build` 2.1.5 the problem goes away, even without re-building the package. 

---

@msarahan commented on [Fri Mar 10 2017](https://github.com/conda/conda-build/issues/1825#issuecomment-285718752)

Sorry, but it looks like a coincidence that conda-build 2.1.5 works but 2.1.6 breaks.  Your build is succeeding (https://travis-ci.org/choderalab/openmmtools/jobs/209789665#L919), but it is only the conda step after that that is broken.  I'm going to move this issue to the conda issue tracker.

Also, this is concerning: https://travis-ci.org/choderalab/openmmtools/jobs/209789665#L925

you should probably add a pin to whichever jpeg you actually want, so that conda does not arbitrarily choose something you don't want.

issue_title: Bug: Variance across trees
issue_body:
Hi,

Maybe I found a bug in the variance prediction.
The following mini example illustrates the issue.
In the example, we have only 2 data points and I train 100 trees until the end (no pruning) without bootstrapping. So, the variance prediction within each tree is zero.

Because of the different split points in each tree, the prediction on x=0.5 is different in each tree. So, 
the variance across trees should be larger than 0.
Unfortunately, the function `predict_mean_var()` nevertheless returns 0 as variance.

@sfalkner Could you please look into this issue.

Thanks,
Marius

```
import numpy as np
from pyrfr import regression

rng = regression.default_random_engine(42)
rf_opts = regression.forest_opts()
rf_opts.num_trees = 100
rf_opts.do_bootstrapping = False
rf_opts.tree_opts.max_features = 1
rf_opts.tree_opts.min_samples_to_split = 1
rf_opts.tree_opts.min_samples_in_leaf = 1
rf_opts.tree_opts.max_depth = 100000
rf_opts.tree_opts.epsilon_purity = 0
rf_opts.tree_opts.max_num_nodes = 100000
rf_opts.num_data_points_per_tree = 2

rf = regression.binary_rss_forest()
rf.options = rf_opts

data = regression.default_data_container(1)

data.set_bounds_of_feature(0,0,1)

data.add_data_point(np.array([0.]), 0.)
data.add_data_point(np.array([1.]), 1.)

rf.fit(data, rng=rng)

m,v = rf.predict_mean_var(np.array([0.5]))

print(m,v)

preds_per_trees = rf.all_leaf_values(np.array([0.5]))
m = np.mean(preds_per_trees, axis=0)
v = np.var(preds_per_trees, axis=0)
print(m,v)```

issue_title: get_implementation_subset dependency fail on components
issue_body:
In some particular situation get_implementation_subset fail to detect correctly the dependency tree. It seems to be related to instantiation by components.
Please find bug test on [my forch](https://github.com/gsorrenti/vunit/tree/master/examples/vhdl/get_implementation_subset_issue).

The conditions are:
1. circular_dependency
The same entity is defined in two different libraries and inside each library it is instantiated by component in others entities. Vunit raise an error of circular dependency.
1. multiple_library_dep
The same entity is compiled in two different libraries. The dependency is propagated to all libraries even if only one should be involved.
1. separated_architecture
In case of entity and architecture definition located in different files, the dependency is not propagated to architecture files.
1. verilog_component
In case of verilog entities instantiated by components, the dependency is not propagated.

issue_title: DNS exports are relative, but include full domain name (missing a dot or omit full domain)
issue_body:
It looks like that **az network dns zone export** is exporting a zone with each record appended by the zone name, however not ending with a . (signalling it's an absolute/fully qualified entry). 

Using the example from pull request #2040, if i exported the DNS record for mycname in zone1.com, this is the exported result ($ORIGIN zone1.com.): 
`mycname.zone1.com 3600 IN CNAME contoso.com.`

However, missing the dot this could mean that it is treated as a relative record, and will then - fully qualified - be interpreted as mycname.zone1.com.zone1.com. This is also true when I import that same zone file/record with **az network dns zone import** (it will create a new record instead of updating an existing one): 
`mycname.zone1.com.zone1.com 3600 IN CNAME contoso.com.`

In that sense, there might be two exports that should/might work (not sure about the exact interpretation rules within the az command): 
fully qualified: `mycname.zone1.com. 3600 IN CNAME contoso.com.`
relative to $ORIGIN: `mycname 3600 IN CNAME contoso.com.`

---

### Environment summary

Azure CLI 2.0 on Windows 10.

Original

from azure.mgmt.storage.models import Encryption, EncryptionServices, EncryptionService

Current

Encryption, EncryptionServices, EncryptionService = versioned_models(ResourceType.MGMT_STORAGE_STORAGE_ACCOUNTS, 'Encryption', 'EncryptionServices', 'EncryptionService')

Before we apply the second pattern wholesale across the CLI, we should investigate how we can approach the ideal of the original, handling the versioning behind this scenes.

This issue is to track _some_ sort of way of enabling an `OR` relationship.

This was originally inspired by https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues/2745

issue_title: Middleware factory is run for every single request
issue_body:
## Long story short
Maybe that's just my unwaranted assumption, but I thought that a middleware factory should run once for every handler, creating the wrapped handlers. Turns out, it's run for every request. This seems to be terribly inefficient.

## Expected behaviour
Run each middleware factory for each handler at one point, at the start of application's lifecycle. Let the new handlers handle requests from that point without being constantly recreated.
   
## Actual behaviour
Middleware factory is run for every request to a handler.

## Steps to reproduce
What I'm creating is a middleware that will parse JWT tokens. I need a mechanism to exculde this parsing on some handlers, since some will be accesses without authorization. I didn't use auihttp_security, since it imposes structure that I don't need (I don't have sessions, for example). I may open-source this code later.

The important bits of my code (with small alterations):

```python
# authorization.py

from functools import wraps

from aiohttp import web
import jwt

NON_SECURED_HANDLERS_KEY = "set of endpoints that don't expect a token"
USER_ID_KEY = 'user ID given by token middleware'

async def token_reading_middleware(app, handler):
    """Reads the token from the authorization header and determines the user ID based on the
    scope inside it.

    Raises 401 if there's something wrong with the header or it's missing.
    
    This check can be disabled by adding the function to the set of non secured handlers
    (on the application object, under `NON_SECURED_HANDLERS_KEY`).
    """
    # assume that APP_CONFIG is imported
    app_secret = app[APP_CONFIG].app_secret

    @wraps(handler)
    async def middleware_handler(request):
        """Adds the user ID parsed from the authorization header to request or raises 401 error.
        """
        # let's say that this function is implemented somewhere and returns 401 is something's wrong with the header
        user_id = _get_user_id_from_auth_header(request, app_secret)
        request[USER_ID_KEY] = user_id
        return await handler(request)

    # this is to tell us that the factory code has been run
    print('XXX', flush=True)
    
    # let's say that this function determines if the handler is esxccepted from security or not
    if _is_except_from_security(handler, app):
        return handler
    return middleware_handler

# app.py
from aiohttp import web

async def _health_check(_):
    return web.HTTPNoContent()


loop =  asyncio.get_event_loop()
app = web.Application(
    loop=loop,
    middlewares=[authorization.token_reading_middleware])
app.router.add_route('GET','/', _health_check)

web.run_app(
    app,
    host='0.0.0.0',
    port=8080,
    loop=loop,
)
```

After starting the app (`python app.py`) I call http://localhost:8080 consecutively. I'm getting 401s because I don;t pass the authorization header.

The problem is, that he application keeps printing 'XXX' for every request, to I know that it jumps through all the hoops of preparing the wrapper and wrapping the handler every time.

## Your environment
AioHTTP 2.2.5
Python 3.6.2
Kubuntu 16.04.3 LTS x64

[bumpversion](https://github.com/peritus/bumpversion) is a python library that allows you to automatically change the version number of a package in all the places that it needs to be changed using a simple CLI.

This would make updating version numbers for releases substantially easier.

General steps:

- [ ] add bumpversion as dependency
- [ ] Identify all places in code where version bumps would be needed [](https://github.com/jupyter/nbgrader/blame/master/nbgrader/docs/source/contributor_guide/releasing.rst#L42-L44)

- [ ] write bumpversion.cfg to match this behaviour.
- [ ] update docs to explain how to use bumpversion

issue_title: list running servers occasionally gives wrong information
issue_body:
`notebook.notebookapp.list_running_servers()` will occasionally give wrong information, listing servers which are no longer running.

I traced this down to having outdated nbserver-xxxx.json info files in my runtime dir. It seems that there is a bug in the `os.unlink` call which tries to remove these old files.

Normally, having the old files hanging around isn't a problem, but on the rare chance that the pid becomes active again from another process, list_running_servers will show that outdated information as a currently running server. This becomes a problem for things like `nbopen()` which attempt to use existing servers rather than spawn new ones.

issue_title: Managing symbol visibility
issue_body:
On windows we need to do this:

```
#  set_target_properties(${_name} PROPERTIES LINK_FLAGS 
#      "/EXPORT:PyInit_${_name}"
#  )
```

On linux, we need to use the linker script to hide symbols:
```
{
  global:
    PyInit_${_name};
  local: *;
}
```

issue_title: Behavior of /v1/wallet/keys/{}
issue_body:
Right now, when you call `PUT /v1/wallet/keys/{}`, the registrar's private key is only _transiently_ set to the given key. This is problematic -- for example, if this method is called a second time before the registrar finishes registration, or if the registrar restarts, any registration still in process moves to a pretty sketchy state.

I plan on solving this by storing the private key being used for account registration in the registrar's queue DB. The SK will be protected by _scrypting_ it with the wallet password (which the registrar already knows).

issue_title: not_unless assertion improvements
issue_body:
not_unless emits warnings via juju-log if a function is called when there is unexpected state.

When running unittests the reactive state will generally not be setup at all. The spurious warnings scribble all over the output of the tests.

Also, in real deploys I'd personally much rather have not_unless fail hard than emit a warning I might not see.

We could add a function that sets a flag to change the behaviour of all the not_unless decorators, handling both of these use cases.

And while we are at it, lets add a not_if decorator to assert state is not set, to compliment not_unless asserting that state is set. I'm only able to make half of the assertions I'd like at the moment.

issue_title: Allow for installing agent from local source tree
issue_body:
_From @hannes-ucsc on April 15, 2015 1:41_

Currently the agent is installed via pip from Github. This means that changes to the agent can't be tested without committing and pushing them. For spark-tools we nest the source tree inside the cgcloud-spark directory and use Fabric's put function to copy that directory to the box and then invoke setup.py on the box. We should offer this mechanism as an alternative to the pip installation, especially in development mode, i.e. when the cgcloud-... packages were installed via `setup.py develop`).

_Copied from original issue: BD2KGenomics/cgcloud-agent#1_

issue_title: Miss module scope coverages when `python setup.py test` sometimes
issue_body:
When working with `pytest`, it is OK. But if working with `python setup.py test` and without pytest-cov installed explicitly, the module scope coverages will be missing.

This bug can be reproducted in any platform. For example:

```sh
git clone https://github.com/yanqd0/csft.git
cd csft
pip install . -r requirements.txt
```

The test result is something like this:

```console
$ python setup.py test
Name               Stmts   Miss  Cover
--------------------------------------
csft/__init__.py      17      7    59%
csft/__main__.py      43     14    67%
csft/_csft.py         52      1    98%
--------------------------------------
TOTAL                112     22    80%
```

This is OK.

But if I uninstall `pytest-cov`, and run `python setup.py test` again, the result will change.

```console
$ pip uninstall pytest-cov -y
Uninstalling pytest-cov-2.5.1:
  Successfully uninstalled pytest-cov-2.5.1
$ python setup.py test
Name               Stmts   Miss  Cover
--------------------------------------
csft/__init__.py      17     17     0%
csft/__main__.py      43     15    65%
csft/_csft.py         52     18    65%
--------------------------------------
TOTAL                112     50    55%
```

The missing parts are all in module scope, like imports and defs.
There is a related demo page in [codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/yanqd0/csft/tree/9ff975d0448b1befb779984ebf0cc26a54c04640/csft).

issue_title: General krb5 functions
issue_body:
My project [python-krbcontext](https://github.com/krbcontext/krbcontext/) relies on python-krbV heavily, and I've been told and suggest to choose python-gssapi, since it's been being developed actively, is compatible with Python 3, and a strong reason is gssapi can do everything krbV does. I have a quick look at gssapi and realize that it implements GSSAPI spec, whereas krbV handles ``context``, ``ccache``, ``principal``, and ``keytab`` with lower level krb5 functions. So, I would like to ask if gssapi has equivalent functionalists, such as

* initialize ``ccache`` in either default or user-specified place
* read ``keytab`` from either default or user-specified place

My project actually doesn't require GSSAPI, because ``krbcontext`` works in the client side of a GSSAPI context, and it is designed to happen before client side code start to call gssapi APIs.

Runtime installation fails on Server Core with WoW64 subsystem removed
issue_body:
On a Windows Server 2016 Core installation with the `WoW64-Support` feature removed, installation of the 64-bit .NET Core runtime fails:

````
PS C:\Users\Administrator> .\dotnet-runtime-2.0.6-win-x64.exe /install /quiet /norestart
Program 'dotnet-runtime-2.0.6-win-x64.exe' failed to run: The application has failed to start because its side-by-side configuration is
incorrect. Please see the application event log or use the command-line sxstrace.exe tool for more detail
````

The Application event log gives more details:

````
PS C:\Users\Administrator> Get-WinEvent -ProviderName SideBySide -MaxEvents 10 | fl *
(...)
Message              : Activation context generation failed for "C:\Users\Administrator\dotnet-runtime-2.0.6-win-x64.exe". Dependent
                       Assembly Microsoft.Windows.Common-Controls,language="*",processorArchitecture="X86",publicKeyToken="6595b64144
                       ccf1df",type="win32",version="6.0.0.0" could not be found. Please use sxstrace.exe for detailed diagnosis.
````

Apparently, the 64-bit .NET Core runtime installer is a 32-bit application and references the 32-bit common controls library, so it fails on servers with the 32-bit subsystem removed.

Steps to reproduce:
- install any Windows Server 2016 Core, for example Hyper-V Server 2016
- start PowerShell
- remove the 32-bit subsystem: 
    `Uninstall-WindowsFeature -Name WoW64-Support -Restart`
- log on and start PowerShell again
- download 64-bit .NET Core runtime 2.0.6 installer: 
    `Invoke-WebRequest -UseBasicParsing -Uri https://download.microsoft.com/download/8/D/A/8DA04DA7-565B-4372-BBCE-D44C7809A467/dotnet-runtime-2.0.6-win-x64.exe -OutFile dotnet-runtime-2.0.6-win-x64.exe`
- attempt installation:
    `.\dotnet-runtime-2.0.6-win-x64.exe /install /quiet /norestart`

Update: corrected download command.

issue_title: How to install the package globally?
issue_body:
My steps are from the getting started guide

```
 git clone https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt
 cd letsencrypt
 ./letsencrypt-auto --help
```
This installs it locally, so if I try to run 

```
 ./letsencrypt-auto --apache
```
After selecting the domain for which to install the certif., I'm getting 

```
failed to connect to host for dvsni challenge
```

By accident I discovered I could run from here, too
```
/root/.local/share/letsencrypt/bin/letsencrypt
```

And this worked just fine. What's the difference between the two?

Running 
```letsencrypt --apache```
like the guide says doesn't work because it is not installed globally by default.

Aside from that, :+1: for this, made my life a bit easier.

issue_title: Apache plugin's cleanup() doesn't remove SSL vhosts created during auth
issue_body: If you run `letsencrypt auth` for an Apache virtual host that doesn't have SSL enabled, the Apache plugin creates a new vhost `*-le-ssl.conf`. After performing the challenges, the plugin's `cleanup` function is called but it doesn't remove the created vhosts. Instead, the user has to run `letsencrypt` again and the files will be deleted up when `recovery_routine` is called. These temporary files should be removed during the `cleanup` function.

issue_title: Support GPG and SSH keys
issue_body:
*securesystemslib* provides an API to import and export public and private keys in PEM (RSA, ECDSA, also cf. #54) or in a proprietary format (Ed25519).

It would be convenient to extend the API so that users can load, e.g. their existing GPG or SSH keys. Note that *securesystemslib* does not necessarily have to provide an API to generate those keys. As @aaaaalbert has mentioned, user might even prefer to use their own well-known toolchain.

issue_body: It would be useful to have some way to tell if the product is supposed to be publicly released.

issue_title: RPMs file format documentation is inconsistent with code
issue_body:
In [the documentation each RPM has a field named `type`](http://release-engineering.github.io/productmd/rpms-1.0.html).

However, when you create the file using the functions in the library and serialize it, the field will be called `category`.

It is possible to parse both with no errors, but the Python representation will obviously be different and cause consuming code to fail.

issue_title: Proper handling of updates
issue_body:
If anyone is interested in collaborating, please refer to the [better-updates](https://github.com/LonamiWebs/Telethon/tree/better-updates) branch. This is an open source project and personally updates don't bother me that much. If you really need updates, feel free to contribute to Telethon instead reinventing the wheel. I will happily merge.

Currently updates are not handled in their best way, duplicates may be received, we never call [`GetDifferenceRequest`](https://lonamiwebs.github.io/Telethon/methods/updates/get_difference.html), and the managing threads or multiple connections should in general be reworked to properly accommodate this feature.

issue_title: switch from recordtype to namedlist or attrs
issue_body:
Originally reported by: **Reece Hart (Bitbucket: [reece](http://bitbucket.org/reece), GitHub: [reece](http://github.com/reece))**

----------------------------------------

Related to: #178, #301

Switch-out the long-deprecated recordtype module. 

Option 1: collections.namedtuple

Option 2: [namedlist](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/namedlist), the successor to recordtype.

Option 3: [attrs](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/attrs/)

recordtype and namedlist are like collections.namedtuple but provides for default values, which are currently used by hgvs.

Another subtle distinction is namedtuple subclasses serialize differently on 2.7 and 3.5, whereas namedlist is consistent.

Consider using namedlist for portability, but drop the use of defaults so that we can swap it out for namedtuple when we're on 3.5 only.

No experience with attrs, but appears to have some nice technical advantages (e.g., optional type checking, optional __slot__ use, creates standard class rather than using class factory).

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/biocommons/hgvs/issue/347

issue_title: sqlalchemy. Declarative support
issue_body:
according to http://flask.pocoo.org/docs/0.10/patterns/sqlalchemy/ , there are two ways documented to use SQLAlchemy in flask, one is with the flask-sqlalchemy extension, the other one is just Declarative, without flask-sqlalchemy. 

For me, I prefer the later one. Because it makes the Database layer much purer. 

In my case, I can extract an models.py file with [sqlacodegen](https://github.com/ksindi/sqlacodegen), and do the migrations with [alembic](https://alembic.readthedocs.io), without `flask-migrate`.

To do the database ops, the `flask` context is not always necessary. and with flask-sqlalchemy, I have to edit the auto-generated models file with all 'db.' prefix to fix the import problems. Well, it brings more cons than pros. 

But I still feel the `ModelSchema` and `HyperlinkModelSchema` are great code savier.

issue_title: Excessive queries on load (nested schemas)
issue_body:
We had this issue for a long time, but now it's starting to become a significant issue for us. See the example below:

```
from flask import Flask
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy, get_debug_queries
from marshmallow import fields
from marshmallow_sqlalchemy import ModelSchema
from datetime import datetime

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:////tmp/test.db'
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_ECHO'] = False
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_TRACK_MODIFICATIONS'] = True
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_RECORD_QUERIES'] = True
db = SQLAlchemy(app)


class User(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = db.Column(db.String(80), unique=True)
    email = db.Column(db.String(120), unique=True)
    created_datetime = db.Column(db.DateTime(), default=datetime.utcnow)
    updated_datetime = db.Column(db.DateTime(), default=datetime.utcnow,
                                 onupdate=datetime.utcnow)

    def __repr__(self):
        return '<User %r>' % self.username


class URL(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey(User.id))
    url = db.Column(db.String())

    user = db.relationship(User, backref=db.backref('urls'))

    def __repr__(self):
        return '<URL %r>' % self.url


def create_user():
    user1 = User(username='user1', email="user1@example.com")
    user1.urls.append(URL(url="http://a"))
    user1.urls.append(URL(url="http://b"))
    user2 = User(username='user2', email="user2@example.com")
    user2.urls.append(URL(url="http://c"))
    user2.urls.append(URL(url="http://d"))

    db.session.add(user1)
    db.session.add(user2)
    db.session.commit()


class UserSchema(ModelSchema):
    urls = fields.Nested('URLSchema', many=True)

    class Meta:
        model = User
        sqla_session = db.session


class URLSchema(ModelSchema):
    class Meta:
        model = URL
        sqla_session = db.session


user_schema = UserSchema()


@app.route("/")
def hello():
    user = User.query.options(db.joinedload('urls')).filter_by(id=1).first()
    user_dump = user_schema.dump(user)
    user_schema.load(user_dump.data, instance=user)
    for query in get_debug_queries():
        print("%s %r\n------" % (query.statement, query.parameters))

    return ''


if __name__ == "__main__":
    db.drop_all()
    db.create_all()
    create_user()
    app.run(port=9999, debug=True)

```

Results in:

```
SELECT anon_1.user_id AS anon_1_user_id, anon_1.user_username AS anon_1_user_username, anon_1.user_email AS anon_1_user_email, anon_1.user_created_datetime AS anon_1_user_created_datetime, anon_1.user_updated_datetime AS anon_1_user_updated_datetime, "URL_1".id AS "URL_1_id", "URL_1".user_id AS "URL_1_user_id", "URL_1".url AS "URL_1_url"
FROM (SELECT user.id AS user_id, user.username AS user_username, user.email AS user_email, user.created_datetime AS user_created_datetime, user.updated_datetime AS user_updated_datetime
FROM user
WHERE user.id = ?
 LIMIT ? OFFSET ?) AS anon_1 LEFT OUTER JOIN "URL" AS "URL_1" ON anon_1.user_id = "URL_1".user_id (1, 1, 0)
------
SELECT user.id AS user_id, user.username AS user_username, user.email AS user_email, user.created_datetime AS user_created_datetime, user.updated_datetime AS user_updated_datetime
FROM user
WHERE user.id = ? (1,)
------
SELECT user.id AS user_id, user.username AS user_username, user.email AS user_email, user.created_datetime AS user_created_datetime, user.updated_datetime AS user_updated_datetime
FROM user
WHERE user.id = ? (1,)
------
SELECT "URL".id AS "URL_id", "URL".user_id AS "URL_user_id", "URL".url AS "URL_url"
FROM "URL"
WHERE "URL".id = ?
 LIMIT ? OFFSET ? (1, 1, 0)
------
SELECT "URL".id AS "URL_id", "URL".user_id AS "URL_user_id", "URL".url AS "URL_url"
FROM "URL"
WHERE "URL".id = ?
 LIMIT ? OFFSET ? (2, 1, 0)
------
127.0.0.1 - - [10/Mar/2017 10:32:47] "GET / HTTP/1.1" 200 -
```

It's a bit hard to parse but you might notice that the `dump` part  is 1 query with an `OUTER JOIN` which is exactly what I expected, however when loading with an attached instance it performs these additional queries.

I think I understand why this happens, you need this because you might need to do an update, but if the instance is given with all the associated objects, the queries above are not really need. 

In an ideal world I would expect: No queries to the DB when doing dump/load when `instance` is provided.

Is there a way to have no side effects when doing `load`? This is a constant source of big bugs for us.

issue_title: AttributeError when pickled function uses submodules
issue_body:
This problem is when a function refers (by attribute) to a sub-module of a package. Cloudpickle appears to pickle functions not by name, but by code plus (a subset of) globals. So the parent package is injected into the pickle, but the sub-module is not. 

```python
def func():
    # import unittest.mock
    x = unittest.TestCase
    x = unittest.mock.Mock
import unittest.mock

import cloudpickle as pickle
s = pickle.dumps(func)

del unittest
import sys
del sys.modules['unittest']
del sys.modules['unittest.mock']

f = pickle.loads(s)
# import unittest.mock as anything
f()
AttributeError: module 'unittest' has no attribute 'mock'
```
This leads to non-intuitive bugs in applications such as cluster computing (e.g. with `dask.distributed`). 
Workarounds:
- perform imports inside functions (contrary to PEP8).
- import sub-modules (or their contents) `as` globals.
- ensure (somehow) that all relevant sub-modules are automatically imported by respective parent packages (e.g. `__init__.py`).
- arrange for the unpickling process to have already done an import of the sub-module (e.g. by first uncloudpickling something else that did refer to the sub-module as a global).

I assume cloudpickle checks whether a global is an imported module, and if so then stores the name (rather than pickling its attributes). Is it practical to also check (via `sys.modules.keys()`) which sub-modules had previously been imported, and ensure every such module is subsequently initialised?

issue_title: Add a new mode to module so accGradInput will override the gradient instead of accumulate
issue_body:
Module has an accGradInput method, which accumulates grad into a tensor. So we need to zero that tensor at the beginning of each iteration. 

This is not necessary if we only accumulate once in each iteration. We can provide a mode flag. If the user set that flag, the accumulating gradient will become overwrite the tensor, which can save the zeroGrad time in the training.

issue_title: Heartbeat race condition for multiple runs
issue_body:
I tested this for FileStorageObserver but it probably can affect other observers as well. Heartbeat race condition can result in observer failure or garbage data in metrics file/records.

### Steps for reproduction (with breakpoints):
1. Execute multiple times (sequentially) Experiment.run() for Experiment single FileStorageObserver
2. Set breakpoint A for last line of IntervalTimer.run() (`self.func()` from line 470 in sacred/utils.py file)
3. Set breakpoint B (enabled after breakpoint A is hit) for FileStorageObserver.log_metrics at loading saved_metrics (`saved_metrics = json.load(open(metrics_path, 'r'))` from line 219 in sacred/observers/file_storage.py file)
4. Resume thread stopped at breakpoint A to breakpoint B

Note that at breakpoint B we have two different Run instances (going through the stack) and same observer (which is already updated - e.g. has changed self.dir - for the second Run). So metrics.json file from second Run will be accessed and modified by both threads. This can result in FileStorageObserver failure (when one thread writes and other tries to read and fails (as json.dump is saved by chunk)) or garbage data in metrics.json.

### Steps for reproduction (without breakpoints):
1. Execute multiple times (sequentially) Experiment.run() under heavy CPU load (e.g. use `dd if=/dev/urandom | pigz  >> /dev/null`).
2. Make sure that saving metrics.json takes some time.
Heavy CPU load and metrics.json saving taking long time can prevent heartbeat thread (IntervalTimer) finishing before next Run starts.


### Original case scenario:
Running Experiments with `multiprocessing.pool.Pool` and reusing processes.

### Problem origin:
`Run._stop_heartbeat()` waits only 2s for heartbeat thread to end and it's sometimes not enough (heavy CPU load complex metrics).

Possible solutions that I can with:
1. Get rid of 2s timeout for joining heartbeat thread (possible infinite waiting if something goes wrong?)
2. Extend timeout and kill thread if still alive (possible data loss)

Workaround:
Every time execute Experiment.run in new process (even for sequential execution)

issue_title: Arbitrary expressions as Layer parameters
issue_body:
Currently, `Layer` parameters are assumed to be Theano shared variables. That way we can call `get_value()` and `set_value()` to them, and pass them to `theano.function` in `updates`.

However, it would be cool if `Layer` parameters could be arbitrary Theano expressions. There are a few use cases for this:

* autoencoders with tied weights. If you have two layers `l1` and `l2`, you might want to do something like `l2 = nntools.layers.DenseLayer(l1, W=l1.W.T)`. This is currently not possible because l1.W.T is not a shared variable, but rather an expression.

* sometimes the domain of parameters is restricted, and you might want to reparameterize things. For example, you might want all values to be positive. In that case you could reparameterize as follows: `l2 = nntools.layers.DenseLayer(l1, W=T.exp(V))`, where V is a shared variable. This ensures that all values in W are positive.

However, this requires some modifications to `nntools.layers.get_all_params()`, because we need a way to get the actual shared variables containing parameter values, not just the Theano expressions built on top of them. Otherwise there's no way to update them.

Given an arbitary Theano expression, it is fairly easy to get a list of all shared variables that occur in it, by traversing the Theano graph. This isn't very 'clean' I suppose, but definitely possible.

However, that just gives us a list of all shared variables, and we have no way of knowing if all of those contain learnable parameters. Perhaps some of them should not be touched by the learning.

We could assume that all shared variables represent learnable parameters by default. This would usually be the case. But then we need to provide a way for the user to specify that a given variable is not to be touched (for example, this could be a variable that contains a binary mask that restricts some of the parameters to be zero). Perhaps an extra attribute on the `Layer` instances that lists all "non-trainable" shared variables.

--

Should we support arbitrary expressions as `Layer` parameters? If so, there will be some added complexity, but it might be worth it. If we do not support this, a new `Layer` subclass has to be implemented for every new parameterization (so to support autoencoders with tied weights, we would need to implement a `TransposedDenseLayer`, for example).

What do you guys think?

issue_title: `CreateFailed` should wrap the underlying error if any
issue_body:
Hi Will, 
while I was working on `fs.sshfs`, I noticed that since `fs.open_fs` is expected to always raise `CreateFailed` when something goes wrong, it's not really easy to report what really went wrong.

I think that the `CreateFailed` exception should probably expose a `exc` attribute, like `OperationFailed` does. This way, it would be easier to manage the failed creation, depending on whether it comes from a `PermissionError`, a `TimeoutError`, a `ResourceNotFound` when `create=False`, etc.

issue_title: Maintenance Wishlist

Hey all, there have been some old parts of desktop that I'd love to revisit. I keep thinking about them as I touch various parts of the app. It's about time I outline what some of them are, either to document what I'm going to go after or see if others want to tackle some. Without further ado:

* [x] Collapse all the (notebook cell) reducers into one that are practically the same `setIn` operation on a cell with a different path. All the action creators will end up creating one kind of action for reduction, and it will simplify any additions to metadata changing. (#1971)
* [x] Merge the `metadata` reducer into the document reducer (which will involve changing the usage)
* [x] Delete the constants.js file, switch to literals. Since we're using flow, we can use the literal type names and last I checked was a requirement for proper flow typing.
* [x] Unify all the action flow types. We've duplicated them across `actions.js` and the reducers
* [x] Convert hover logic for the cell creator to CSS (#1972)

s1.run()

issue_title: CFGFast: infinite loop
issue_body:
I figured that, at least for PE files, CFGFast might get stuck in an infinite loop.
The code causing the trouble starts here:
https://github.com/angr/angr/blob/2f718925e2f6e3cd109075f4d798ca5130092681/angr/analyses/cfg_fast.py#L613

Since the `get_max_addr()` of the PE module already takes relocation into account, `self._end` is far beyond the mapped memory.

Later in the analyses process `_next_code_address` calls `_next_unscanned_addr`, which in turn returns `self._end`, which is behind any mapped memory.
The infinite loop starts at: 
https://github.com/angr/angr/blob/2f718925e2f6e3cd109075f4d798ca5130092681/angr/analyses/cfg_fast.py#L786
because 
```python
 while next_addr in self._initial_state.memory:
```
will never be true, the conditional check
```python
if len(sz) > 0 and is_sz:
```
is always false (since sz is still empty), and https://github.com/angr/angr/blob/2f718925e2f6e3cd109075f4d798ca5130092681/angr/analyses/cfg_fast.py#L819 causes the loop to repeat with an address even further out of the mapped image.

Is this a problem of the PE module or is the initial calculation of `self._end` wrong?

issue_title: BackendVSA doesn't handle `If` expressions
issue_body:
When a `RegionAnnotation` is applied to a `BV`, `BackendVSA` transforms it into a `ValueSet`, excep if it contains an `If` expression:

```python
import claripy
si0 = claripy.SI('si0')

si1 = claripy.SI('si1')
si2 = claripy.SI('si2')
si12 = claripy.If(claripy.BVS('x',1), si1, si2)

print type(si0.annotate(claripy.vsa.RegionAnnotation('region0',0,0))._model_vsa)
# <class 'claripy.vsa.valueset.ValueSet'>

print type(si12.annotate(claripy.vsa.RegionAnnotation('region12',0,0))._model_vsa)
# <class 'claripy.vsa.strided_interval.StridedInterval'>
```
